titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Alan Hollinghurst Papers
titlestmt.author: by Emma Gronbeck

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday June 3, 2023 at 05:00

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection contains correspondence, writings, printed material, and electronic media created or collected by English novelist, editor, and translator Alan Hollinghurst. Drafts of Hollinghurst's first four novels, which chronicle gay life in Great Britain, form the bulk of the collection. Also included are drafts of other short writings and unpublished works, a translation of Jean Racine's Bajazet , and screenplay adaptations of The Swimming Pool Library and The Line of Beauty .

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 14 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Alan Hollinghurst and John Byrne on the James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection Fund, 2019.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Alan Hollinghurst Papers. James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains correspondence, writings, printed material, and electronic media created or collected by English novelist, editor, and translator Alan Hollinghurst. Drafts of Hollinghurst's first four novels, which chronicle gay life in Great Britain, form the bulk of the collection. Also included are drafts of other short writings and unpublished works, a translation of Jean Racine's Bajazet , and screenplay adaptations of The Swimming Pool Library and The Line of Beauty .

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Alan Hollinghurst Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

bioghist.head: Alan Hollinghurst (1954-)
bioghist.p: Alan Hollinghurst is an English novelist, poet, short story writer, and translator. His works often chronicle gay life in Great Britain and include The Line of Beauty , winner of the 2004 Booker Prize. Hollinghurst was born on May 26, 1954, in Stroud, Gloucestershire, to James and Elizabeth Hollinghurst and attended Magdalene College, Oxford, receiving a B.A. in English in 1975 and a Master of Letters in 1979. As an undergraduate, he won the Newdigate Prize for poetry in 1974. Hollinghurst received the 1989 Somerset Maugham Award for The Swimming Pool Library (1988) and the 1994 James Tait Black Memorial Prize for The Folding Star . Other notable works are The Spell (1988) and The Stranger's Child (2011), which was longlisted for the Booker Prize. In 2011, Hollinghurst received the Bill Whitehead Award.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. Correspondence, 1987-2006. II. Writings, 1980-2006. III. Printed Material, 1988-2005. IV. Computer Media, 2002-2006.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit. Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing. This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of correspondence with Alan Hollinghurst's friends, family, fans, and professional contacts. Letters to Hollinghurst primarily offer commentary on his work or congratulations on the publication of one of his novels. Also indcluded are a small amount of photographs.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by correspondent, with unidentified correspondence and photographs filed at the end of the sequence.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Correspondence can also be found in Series II. Writings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of drafts, proofs, notes, reviews, awards, and correspondence related to the publication and promotion of Hollinghurst's first four published novels, The Swimming Pool Library (1988), The Folding Star (1994), The Spell (1998), and The Line of Beauty (2004). Drafts and proofs of shorter works, such as book reviews, short stories, articles, and translations are also included, as well as drafts of an early, unpublished novel titled Dead Letters .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically by date of publication.

did.unittitle: Ronald Firbank , draft and correspondence with Peter Eyre
The Flower Beneath the Foot by Ronald Firbank, draft edited by Alan Hollinghurst
"Alan Hollinghurst Writes About the Poems and Novels of the Author of The White Hotel ," proofs
Institute of Contemporary Arts Gallery piece, research on Robert Mapplethorpe
Correspondence, Chatto & Windus and The Hogarth Press
Writings of others about The Swimming Pool Library
Leuven, K. U. "Unsolicited Remarks: A Reading of Alan Hollinghurst's The Swimming Pool Library "
Screenplay adaptation by Kevin Elyot, Episode 1, draft
Screenplay adaptation by Kevin Elyot, Episode 2, draft
Screenplay adaptation by Kevin Elyot, Episode 3, draft
Bajazet by Jean Racine, translation by Alan Hollinghurst
Correspondence, Chatto & Windus and The Hogarth Press
The International IMPAC Dublin Literary Award
Writings of others about The Folding Star
Weher-Pinkowski, Marion, "It Isn't Another World: Alan Hollinghurst's Novels The Swimming Pool Library and The Folding Star "
"I Often Laugh When I'm Alone: The Novels of Ronald Firbank," proofs and correspondence
A. E. Housman: poems selected by Alan Hollinghurst , correspondence and drafts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains books gifted to Alan Hollinghurst, publishing catalogs featuring his novels, and journals, magazines, and newspapers containing interviews or reviews of Hollinghurst's work.

did.unittitle: L'Alcibiade fanciullo a scolu by Antonio Rocco
Elixir of Empire by P. J. Rich

title.part: Songs with a Dying Fall by David Schorr

did.unittitle: Chatto & Windus and The Hogarth Press

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 3 1/2-inch floppy disks, compact disks, and DVDs containing drafts of Hollinghurst's works and copies of all three episodes of the BBC television adaptation of The Line of Beauty .

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 14 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
